title: Logging System — File-Based Logs
description: Create a Logger class that writes timestamped log entries to a file. It should support info(), error(), and warning() methods, each writing a line in the format '[TIMESTAMP] LEVEL: message'. Use file I/O in append mode. Include log rotation: when the log file exceeds max_bytes, rename it to logfile.1 and start a new file. The Logger should accept a file path and optional max_bytes (default 1MB) in its constructor.
test_code:
# Tests for Step 3: Logger
import os
import tempfile

assert 'Logger' in dir(), 'Logger class must be defined'

with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
    log_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, 'test.log')
    logger = Logger(log_path, max_bytes=500)

    # Test info method
    logger.info('Server started')
    assert os.path.exists(log_path), 'Log file should be created'

    with open(log_path, 'r') as f:
        content = f.read()
    assert 'INFO' in content, 'Log should contain INFO level'
    assert 'Server started' in content, 'Log should contain the message'
    assert '[' in content and ']' in content, 'Log should have timestamp brackets'

    # Test error method
    logger.error('Connection failed')
    with open(log_path, 'r') as f:
        content = f.read()
    assert 'ERROR' in content, 'Log should contain ERROR level'
    assert 'Connection failed' in content, 'Log should contain error message'

    # Test warning method
    logger.warning('Disk space low')
    with open(log_path, 'r') as f:
        content = f.read()
    assert 'WARNING' in content, 'Log should contain WARNING level'

    # Test that all three methods are callable
    assert callable(getattr(logger, 'info', None)), 'info must be callable'
    assert callable(getattr(logger, 'error', None)), 'error must be callable'
    assert callable(getattr(logger, 'warning', None)), 'warning must be callable'

    # Test log rotation (write enough to exceed max_bytes=500)
    for i in range(50):
        logger.info(f'Filling up the log file with message number {i}')
    rotated_path = log_path + '.1'
    assert os.path.exists(rotated_path), 'Rotated log file (.1) should exist after exceeding max_bytes'
acceptance_criteria: Logger class accepts log_path and optional max_bytes
info(), error(), and warning() methods write formatted log entries
Log entries include timestamps in [YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS] format
Log entries include the level (INFO, ERROR, WARNING)
Files are opened in append mode
Log rotation renames the file to .1 when size exceeds max_bytes
hints: Check file size with os.path.getsize(self.log_path) before deciding to rotate.
Use os.replace(self.log_path, self.log_path + '.1') for atomic rename on rotation.
